steps.title: Keep the phrase unified
steps.content: The sentence says barter economies eschew currency in favor of direct trade. The object and the prepositional phrase form one continuous idea, so no punctuation should interrupt them.

steps.title: Reject unnecessary breaks
steps.content: Choice A places a comma between currency and the phrase that completes the contrast. Choice B uses a dash for the same unnecessary break. Choice D adds both an internal comma and a trailing comma. The unpunctuated wording preserves the standard expression. The answer is C.
